informal: Prove that if $|G|=105$ then $G$ has a normal Sylow 5 -subgroup and a normal Sylow 7-subgroup.
lean:
theorem Dummit_Foote_exercise_4_5_17 {G : Type*} [Fintype G] [Group G]
  (hG : card G = 105) :
  (∃ (P : Sylow 5 G), P.Normal) ∧ (∃ (P : Sylow 7 G), P.Normal)
reasoning: The Lean signature assumes a finite group G with |G| = 105 and concludes the existence of a Sylow 5-subgroup that is normal and a Sylow 7-subgroup that is normal. Since all Sylow p-subgroups of a finite group are conjugate, the existence of one normal Sylow p-subgroup is equivalent to "G has a normal Sylow p-subgroup" in the informal sense (and equivalently, the Sylow p-subgroup is unique). The hypothesis `card G = 105` together with `Fintype G` and `Group G` matches "|G| = 105". The conjunction matches the two conclusions of the informal statement. No extra hypotheses are imposed and none are dropped.
